fallback: Used book2skill/skill-creator-style distillation because no local cangjie executable was available in this runtime. The assigned DRS supplement source was mostly site navigation/footer text, so book metadata and theory content were supplemented from local metadata.json and the MIT Press book page.

supplementedMetadata.representativeWork: Herbert A. Simon, The Sciences of the Artificial
supplementedMetadata.editionContext: Reissue of the third edition, originally published in 1996, with a new introduction by John E. Laird

runtimeDependencyPolicy: Closed skill: execute from SKILL.md; use references/source-notes.md only as local optional provenance; do not require external source folders, webpages, original books, source reports, machine-local paths, or cross-skill files.

closureReview.remainingRisk: Source provenance includes supplemented book metadata; runtime dependency on the previously referenced notes file was removed from SKILL.md.

qualityChecks: SKILL.md states self-contained runtime contract and no longer asks runtime agents to read source-notes.
External source paths are preserved only in sourceFiles for audit trace.
test-prompts.json includes should_trigger, should_not_trigger, edge_cases, and compliance_expectations.
